title: Broken Object Level Authorization
description: APIs expose endpoints that handle object identifiers, creating a wide attack surface of Object Level Access Control issues. Object level authorization checks should be considered in every function that accesses a data source using an ID from the user.
check_pattern: endpoints with path parameters lack 401 or 403 responses
fix_guidance: Implement object-level authorization checks for every endpoint that receives an ID. Use GUIDs instead of numeric IDs. Write tests to evaluate the authorization mechanism.

title: Broken Authentication
description: Authentication mechanisms are often implemented incorrectly, allowing attackers to compromise authentication tokens or exploit implementation flaws to assume other users' identities.
check_pattern: missing authentication scheme, no OAuth2 or API key defined, missing security definitions
fix_guidance: Define authentication schemes in the securitySchemes section. Apply security requirements globally or per-endpoint. Ensure 401 responses are defined for all authenticated endpoints.

title: Broken Object Property Level Authorization
description: APIs expose more object properties than required for the business logic of the endpoint, leading to excessive data exposure or mass assignment vulnerabilities.
check_pattern: response schemas expose sensitive fields without filtering, request bodies accept all properties
fix_guidance: Define explicit response schemas that only include necessary fields. Use read-only and write-only property modifiers. Never return sensitive fields like passwords or tokens.

title: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
description: API requests consume resources such as network bandwidth, CPU, memory, and storage. Without rate limiting, APIs are vulnerable to denial of service attacks.
check_pattern: missing 429 response code, no rate limit headers defined, no pagination on collection endpoints
fix_guidance: Add 429 Too Many Requests response to all endpoints. Document rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining). Implement pagination for collection endpoints.

title: Broken Function Level Authorization
description: Complex access control policies with different roles and groups tend to lead to authorization flaws. Administrative endpoints are exposed to regular users.
check_pattern: admin or management endpoints missing 403 responses, no scope-based security defined
fix_guidance: Define OAuth2 scopes for different access levels. Apply specific security requirements to admin endpoints. Document 403 Forbidden responses for all role-restricted endpoints.

title: Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
description: APIs expose business flows without compensating controls that protect against excessive automated use, leading to business logic abuse.
check_pattern: checkout, login, signup endpoints missing rate limiting or 429 responses
fix_guidance: Add rate limiting to sensitive business flows. Implement CAPTCHA or device fingerprinting. Document expected usage patterns and add 429 responses.

title: Server Side Request Forgery
description: SSRF flaws occur when an API fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URI.
check_pattern: endpoints accepting URL parameters or callback URIs without validation documentation
fix_guidance: Document input validation for URL parameters. Define allowlist of acceptable URL patterns. Add schema validation with format and pattern constraints.

title: Security Misconfiguration
description: APIs and supporting systems contain security misconfigurations, including permissive cross-origin resource sharing, verbose error messages, and unnecessary HTTP methods.
check_pattern: missing CORS configuration, verbose error responses exposing stack traces, unnecessary HTTP methods enabled
fix_guidance: Document CORS policies. Define standardized error response schemas that do not expose internal details. Only define required HTTP methods per endpoint.

title: Improper Inventory Management
description: APIs expose deprecated or unreleased API versions without proper lifecycle management, creating unnecessary attack surfaces.
check_pattern: missing API versioning in paths, no deprecation markers, missing info version field
fix_guidance: Include API version in all paths (e.g., /v1/). Use the deprecated flag on obsolete endpoints. Maintain an API changelog in the info description.

title: Unsafe Consumption of APIs
description: Developers tend to trust data received from third-party APIs without validating or sanitizing it, enabling injection attacks.
check_pattern: third-party API responses not validated, missing schema constraints on external data
fix_guidance: Define strict schema validation for all request and response bodies. Use enum constraints, pattern matching, and min/max length on string fields.
